WHITE COAT FORGE
  • Entry
  • Program
  • Curriculum
  • Progression

Program Requirements

Learning, teaching, and assessment — what's required and when.

Learning Program

Clinical Experience

36 months full-time equivalent (FTE) in a mix of approved training rotations — in any sequence over the course of training.

Rotation Plans

1 rotation plan per training rotation, submitted at the start of each rotation.

  • Due 28 February — for rotations in the first half of the year
  • Due 31 August — for rotations in the second half of the year

Teaching Program

The teaching program guides supervisors, assessors, and accredited training settings to plan and deliver teaching activities.

Required Supervision Roles

Each trainee must have named individuals or entities for the following roles (people may hold multiple roles):

  • Network Director where a network exists
  • Director of Physician Education
  • Progress Review Panel
  • Education Supervisor
  • Rotation Supervisor

Assessment Program

Minimum requirements per phase of training (each year):

12
Learning captures — min. 1 per month
12
Observation captures — min. 1 per month

Rotation Progress Reports

1 report per training rotation (minimum one per three months), completed at the end of each rotation.

Example

5 training rotations in Foundation phase → 5 rotation progress reports

2 × 6-month rotations in Foundation phase → 4 rotation progress reports (1 per 3 months)

Phase Progress Reports

2 per phase — 1 mid-phase report and 1 end-of-phase report.

Examinations

Written Examination

1 — early in the Completion phase

Clinical Examination

1 — mid-way through the Completion phase

These are minimum requirements. More work-based assessments are encouraged. Directors of Physician Education and Education Supervisors may require additional assessments.

White Coat Forge — RACP Basic Training Reference  ·  Not affiliated with RACP
